title: Validate at the boundary, trust internal code
description: Added defensive null-checks and type-guards on every function in a module. The code became unreadable, and a real bug (an actually-null value from a third-party API) still got through because the checks were in the wrong place — five layers deep, after the value had already been used.
context: Internal module call chain where the caller is fully under our control.
resolution: Removed the internal guards, added one schema validation at the API ingress, and gave internal functions the types they always should have had.
prevention: Validate untrusted input ONCE at the system boundary (HTTP handler, queue consumer, file parser, CLI argument parsing). Inside the boundary, trust the types. Don't add null-checks for situations that the type system says can't happen.
trigger: About to add a null-check or type-guard inside an internal-only module
